task_completion.description: Open the NCH Copper Point of Sale application
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main window of the NCH Copper Point of Sale software is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Access the database backup feature within the application
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Back Up Data', 'Backup', or similar dialog box is open within the Copper POS application.

task_completion.description: Set the backup destination directory to the required path
task_completion.visual_evidence: The path 'C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\POS_Backup' is visible in the destination folder field of the backup dialog.

task_completion.description: Initiate and complete the backup process
task_completion.visual_evidence: A progress bar, success message, or confirmation dialog indicating that the backup has been successfully completed.

task_completion.description: Verify the backup folder was created in the destination directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: A File Explorer window open to 'C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\POS_Backup' showing a newly created folder named 'Copper-Backup-YYYY-MM-DD' (where YYYY-MM-DD is the current date).

integrity.description: The agent used the Copper POS application GUI to perform the backup
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the Copper POS menus and backup dialogs, rather than manual file copying in File Explorer.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to bypass the application
integrity.visual_evidence: No Command Prompt, PowerShell, or terminal windows should be visible executing 'cp', 'xcopy', or 'mkdir' commands to create the backup.

integrity.description: The backup folder was generated by the software, not manually created
integrity.visual_evidence: The presence of a success dialog from the Copper POS software confirming the backup, rather than the agent just right-clicking and creating an empty folder named 'Copper-Backup-YYYY-MM-DD'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: NCH Copper Point of Sale performs backups by creating a folder named 'Copper-Backup-YYYY-MM-DD' (rather than a single file with extensions like .zip or .bak). The correct destination directory is C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\POS_Backup.
